ogTitle: Valley Heights vs Vaucluse: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Valley Heights and Vaucluse.
heading: Valley Heights vs Vaucluse — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Valley Heights, New South Wales and Vaucluse, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Valley Heights has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Vaucluse (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Valley Heights or Vaucluse safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Valley Heights has a flood risk score of 20/100, while Vaucluse scores 51/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Valley Heights scores 35/100 for bushfire risk. Vaucluse scores 28/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Valley Heights or Vaucluse?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Valley Heights has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.25% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
